name: Clay Pot Chicken & Vegetables in Herbs & Wine
recipeIngredient: 1 pound red potatoes, small and halved
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 3 cloves garlic, thyme, and rosemary in the cavity of the chicken.
2. Truss the chicken and place it in a ziplock bag.
3. Pour in wine and refrigerate overnight, turning occasionally.
4. Soak the top and bottom of the pot in cold water for 30 minutes.
5. Cook bacon over medium heat until brown and crisp. Drain on paper towels and reserve drippings.
6. Drain the pot. Remove chicken from the bag and place breast side up in the pot.
7. Add wine, bacon pieces, and sprinkle chicken with half of salt and pepper. Cover with the top and place on the bottom shelf in the cold oven.
8. Set temperature to 450 degrees and cook for 1 1/2 hours.
9. About 30 minutes before the chicken is done, toss potatoes, carrots, celery root, and onions with butter and reserved bacon drippings, and remaining salt and pepper in a shallow baking pan. Place on the upper shelf in the oven; cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
10. Add mushrooms and cook for an additional 5 minutes until tender and lightly brown.
11. When chicken is done, remove from the oven. Remove chicken to a platter and pour cooking liquids into a saucepan, skimming off fat.
12. Stir cornstarch into cooking liquids over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil and reduce heat to medium-low. Cook for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally until slightly thickened.
13. Serve chicken and vegetables in the clay pot with sauce on the side.
